Assumption: A new user can understand the system and create their first product record in ten minutes.
Smallest Test: Give the duplicated template and quick-start page to three people without a live walkthrough.
Success Signal: At least two of three create a product, idea, and active week in under ten minutes.
Follow-up: Recruit three testers and record completion time.

Assumption: Builders need active weekly work above the full idea backlog.
Smallest Test: Compare a dashboard with This Week first against one with Ideas first.
Success Signal: Testers identify the current priority faster with This Week first.
Result: Early self-test favors the weekly-first layout.
Learning: Active work should stay above reference and backlog views.

Assumption: A lightweight proof inbox feels immediately useful to product builders.
Smallest Test: Add three proof examples and ask a builder where they would save a customer quote.
Success Signal: Builder chooses the Proof Library without prompting.
Result: Builder chose the Proof Library without prompting.
Learning: The category names are understandable when examples are present.
Follow-up: Keep example proof records in the sellable template.
